label: Year 0 HE: Göbekli Tepe
description: Construction of the world's first temple in modern-day Turkey. Hunter-gatherers gather to build a massive stone monument, marking the start of the Human Era (HE).

label: Year 1,000 HE: First City Walls
description: Jericho begins to take shape as one of the first permanent human settlements with stone walls.

label: Year 2,000 HE: Agriculture Widespread
description: Humans transition from nomadic lifestyles to permanent farming. The global population is roughly 5 million.

label: Year 3,000 HE: Pottery & Storage
description: Widespread use of pottery allows for better food storage and early industrial production.

label: Year 4,000 HE: Early Metalworking
description: Humans begin processing copper and tin, leading to the first metal tools.

label: Year 7,000 HE: Writing & The Wheel
description: Invention of writing in Sumer and Egypt. The wheel is developed for transport. Construction of the Great Pyramid and Stonehenge begins.

label: Year 8,500 HE: The Iron Age
description: Iron replaces bronze, leading to more durable tools and larger-scale civilizations.

label: Year 9,500 HE: The Classical Age
description: Rise of Greek city-states, the Persian Empire, and the teachings of Buddha and Confucius.

label: Year 10,000 HE: Common Era Begins
description: The traditional Year 1 of the Gregorian calendar. By this point, 10,000 years of human progress have already passed.

label: Year 11,750 HE: Industrial Revolution
description: Steam power and factories fundamentally change the human relationship with the planet.

label: Year 11,969 HE: Moon Landing
description: Apollo 11: Humans leave Earth and set foot on another celestial body for the first time.

label: Year 12,000 HE: The Digital Age
description: The rise of the Internet and global digital connectivity.

label: Year 12,026 HE: Today
description: We live in the most prosperous and technologically advanced age in human history.
